zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Maud Deincourt於1275年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾艾姆利時，她的父親Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourt26歲，母親Joan Clinton-25歲。她於1296年與William FitzWilliam在英國英格蘭越可爾艾姆利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1348年5月29日在英國英格蘭越可爾享年73歲，埋葬在英國越可爾斯普洛特布盧 安德 庫斯沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Maud Deincourt于1275年出生在英国英格兰越可爾艾姆利时，她的父亲Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourt26岁，母亲Joan Clinton-25岁。她于1296年与William FitzWilliam在英国英格兰越可爾艾姆利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1348年5月29日在英国英格兰越可爾享年73岁，埋葬在英国越可爾斯普洛特布盧 安德 庫斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Maud Deincourt was born in 1275, in Emley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourt, was 26 and her mother, Joan Clinton, was -25. She married William FitzWilliam in 1296, in Emley, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 29 May 1348, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in Sprotbrough and Cusworth, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Maud Deincourt est née en 1275, à Emley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourt, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Joan Clinton, avait -25 ans. Elle a épousé William FitzWilliam en 1296, à Emley, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 mai 1348, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Sprotbrough and Cusworth, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Maud Deincourtが1275年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアエムリーで生まれた時，父Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourtは26歳，母Joan Clintonは-25歳であった。1296年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアエムリーでWilliam FitzWilliamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1348年5月29日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで73歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアスプロトブロウ アンド クウスウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Maud Deincourt(이)가 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourt(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Clinton(은)는 -25세였다. 그녀는 1296년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리에서 William FitzWilliam(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 5월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 스플로트브루그h 앤드 커스워스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Maud Deincourt родилась в 1275 г. в местности эмли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Edmund Deincourt 1st Baron Deincourt было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Clinton, -25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William FitzWilliam в 1296 г. в местности эмли, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 29 мая 1348 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности спротброую анд кусворт, еркшир, Англия.

Se casó con William FitzWilliam en 1296, en Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 29 de mayo de 1348, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Sprotbrough and Cusworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
